sanskrit: यथा नदीनां बहवोऽम्बुवेगाः समुद्रमेवाभिमुखा द्रवन्ति। तथा तवामी नरलोकवीरा विशन्ति वक्त्राण्यभिविज्वलन्ति॥
transliteration: yathā nadīnāṃ bahavo 'mbu-vegāḥ samudram evābhimukhā dravanti / tathā tavāmī nara-loka-vīrā viśanti vaktrāṇy abhivijvalanti
literal_meaning: As the many waves of rivers rush toward the ocean, so these heroes of the human world enter Your blazing mouths.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna sees every heroic figure he has ever known—friends, mentors, rivals—being swallowed by Time’s fiery jaws. The image externalizes his fear that conflict will annihilate not only bodies but the entire moral order he relies on.
psychological_state: Traumatic dissociation: the mind converts overwhelming anxiety into a panoramic vision of mass destruction, allowing the self to observe rather than feel annihilation directly.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling through real-time footage of a natural disaster or war zone and recognizing the faces of people you know among the victims.
anchor_text: Let go of clinging to how things should be and meet change with steady presence.
